search_queries_used: site:bsaci.org rhinitis postnasal drip throat examination guideline
site:brit-thoracic.org.uk chronic cough upper airway symptoms 2023
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic upper-airway-cough-syndrome, sinusitis, allergy, or reflux diagnosis
automatic antihistamine, steroid, saline, reflux medicine, imaging, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Postnasal drip is a symptom and does not establish the underlying cause.
No investigation, medicine, dose, treatment, procedure, or referral is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Children or adults with postnasal-drip sensation in a possible rhinitis context; chronic adult cough evidence is age-limited and children require separate assessment.
setting_applicability: Primary, allergy, respiratory, or ENT assessment.
UAE_applicability: BSACI and BTS evidence require UAE allergy, respiratory, infection, prescribing, ENT, and referral adaptation.
recency_verification: The official BSACI 2017 and BTS 2023 exact sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: BTS 2023 remains current; BSACI 2017 remains listed with recency limitations.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact rhinitis history section supports onset, nasal discharge, throat clearing or cough context, triggers, obstruction, sleep impact, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, and concerns without asserting rhinitis, reflux, or an upper-airway cough cause.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting onset, duration, triggers, rhinorrhoea, obstruction, sneezing, itching, crusting, eye and lower-airway symptoms, sleep, snoring, mouth breathing, family, social, occupational, and medicine context without assigning rhinitis subtype.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact examination section supports clinician-performed nasal, throat, secretion, crusting, polyp, and postnasal-drip assessment without auto-populating findings.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-performed general inspection, mouth-breathing observation, airflow assessment, anterior rhinoscopy, secretions, septal appearance, polyps, crusting, throat, and postnasal-drip documentation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact investigation context supports review of existing specialist information and clinician-entered plan or follow-up without ordering tests or recommending medicine.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports recording clinician-selected allergy testing and already available specialist or imaging results; routine radiology and autonomous testing are not mapped.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact chronic-cough history section was reviewed for adult cough and upper-airway context, but no cough diagnosis or treatment text was mapped.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports cough duration and character, sputum, smoking, medicines, occupation and environment, wheeze, rhinitis, heartburn, functional and mood impact, and face-to-face upper-airway, ear and chest examination.

unresolved_source_gaps: Duration, nasal discharge, cough, throat clearing, allergy, infection, reflux, smoking, medicines, examination, imaging, diagnosis, treatment, and referral remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
